title: athlete — subprocessors
description: The Athlete module itself engages ZERO third-party subprocessors — every state transition happens within the Academorix platform. The Notifications module dispatches athlete lifecycle notifications via its own subprocessors (mail, SMS, push); those are declared in notifications::subprocessors.json. The Storage module (Wave 4+) will engage object-storage subprocessors for profile_photo_url upload workflow; declared in storage::subprocessors.json. Third-party medical-clearance verification providers (Wave 4+ Safeguarding module) will engage medical-record subprocessors; declared there. This file lists ZERO active subprocessors to be explicit about the module's boundary — athlete state is fully first-party.

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.used_for: Dispatching AthleteWelcome / AthleteGraduated / AthleteWithdrawn / AthleteMinorTurnedAdult / AthleteConsentExpiring / AthleteAgeGroupSnapshotChanged / AthletePausedForSafeguarding / AthleteConsentRecorded notifications via mail / SMS / push channels.
profile_photo_url upload workflow when the Storage module introduces the /media endpoints.
MedicalClearance workflow (Wave 4+) may engage third-party medical-verification services for high-safeguarding-tier tenants (schools, government-contract sports academies). Not applicable Wave 3a.

notes: This file exists deliberately even with an empty subprocessors array. The GDPR Art. 30 record of processing activities requires per-module subprocessor lists; an empty list is the correct declaration for a module that engages none directly.

If a future Wave introduces a subprocessor into this module (e.g. an AI-based age-group anomaly detector or a medical-history-scanning service), add it here + update compliance.json Art. 28 evidence + refresh the tenant-facing subprocessor list.
